title: Koning Hendrik de Zesde
summaries: "Koning Hendrik de Zesde" by William Shakespeare is a trilogy of history plays written in the late 16th century. The three plays chronicle the turbulent reign of King Henry VI of England, from the loss of French territories through the devastating Wars of the Roses. They depict a kingdom torn apart by political scheming, personal rivalries, and the king's inability to control his quarreling nobles, culminating in brutal armed conflict that would reshape English history.
